channel.title: Yahoo News – World
channel.description: Latest world news from Yahoo News

item.title: Zelensky warns of new large-scale Russian attacks, urges precautions
item.description: Ukrainian President Volodymyr Zelensky warned on Saturday evening that Russia was preparing new large-scale attacks and urged citizens to take precautions. Speaking in his nightly video address to the nation, Zelensky pointed to strikes on the cities of Dnipro and Zaporizhzhya hours earlier, saying that Moscow now appeared to be preparing for more assaults overnight.
item.dc:creator: dpa

item.title: Lebanese turtle conservationist Mona Khalil killed by Israeli strike
item.description: Mona Khalil, who had refused to leave the beach she had spent years protecting, died from her injuries after the Israeli strike.
item.dc:creator: BBC

item.title: Bird survives arrow shot through neck, rescued by German firefighters
item.description: Firefighters in the west German city of Kleve close to the Dutch border have rescued a bird that had been shot with an arrow that lodged in its neck, the Kleve fire brigade said on Saturday. Despite the injury, the bird was still swimming briskly on a lake on Friday, the emergency services said.
item.dc:creator: dpa

item.title: Driver dies in England train crash, 9 still critical
item.description: A train driver is dead and nine people are still in critical condition after two passenger trains collided in England. About 80 people were injured.
item.dc:creator: UPI

item.title: Greece battles first major wildfire of the year
item.description: A major wildfire on the Greek island of Euboea - the country's first this year - prompted authorities to order precautionary evacuations on Saturday as strong winds drove the flames towards residential areas. The fire broke out near the villages of Mesochoria and Raptei in the afternoon, with the fire service urging residents to leave the area.
item.dc:creator: dpa

item.title: Al Jazeera cameraman Ahmed Wishah killed in Israeli attack in Gaza
item.description: Al Jazeera said it ‘strongly condemns the heinous crime of targeting and killing’ the Al Jazeera Mubasher correspondent.
item.dc:creator: Al Jazeera

item.title: Middle East flights reopen as new confusion hits travelers
item.description: The Iran war triggered airspace closures, grounding tens of thousands of flights and cutting off key global transit hubs.
item.dc:creator: Newsweek US

item.title: Russian strike on Ukraine's Zaporizhzhya kills four
item.description: At least four people were killed and six injured in a Russian airstrike on the south-eastern Ukrainian city of Zaporizhzhya, regional authorities said on Saturday. Regional military governor Ivan Fedorov said on Telegram that nine aerial bombs struck the city centre.
item.dc:creator: dpa

item.title: Zelensky returns highest Polish honour as historical row deepens
item.description: Ukrainian President Volodymyr Zelensky has returned Poland's highest state honour amid an escalating historical dispute between the two allies, after Polish President Karol Nawrocki stripped him of the award. Zelensky said on X he had sent back the Order of the White Eagle, which he received in 2023 as a symbol of friendship between Poland and Ukraine.
item.dc:creator: dpa

item.title: Russia frees 24 Filipinos after Philippine President Marcos talks with Putin
item.description: There were reports that the Filipinos may have been victims of illegal job recruitment, Philippine officials said.
item.dc:creator: CBS News

item.title: US forces monitoring Strait of Hormuz to ensure it stays open
item.description: WASHINGTON, June 20 (Reuters) - The U.S. military on Saturday denied Iran's claims that it had closed the Strait of Hormuz, saying the critical waterway remained open and that U.S. forces were
item.dc:creator: Reuters

item.title: At least one tourist dead after massive fire nearly destroys Caribbean resort, 1,690 evacuated
item.description: An Italian tourist died and nine others were injured after a massive fire swept through a Dominican Republic resort in Bayahibe, authorities say.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Iran says it's closing Strait of Hormuz and accuses US and Israel of violating ceasefire deal
item.description: Iran's military says it's blocking the vital waterway over Israeli strikes in Lebanon, but the US military says passage in the strait "remained intact".

item.title: As ILO convention turns 30, India’s home-based workers demand equal rights
item.description: Convention 177 was adopted on 20 June 1996, recognising home-based workers on a par with traditional wage earners.

item.title: Woman killed in stabbing rampage at Russian shopping centre
item.description: A woman was killed and several other people were injured in a stabbing rampage at a shopping centre in the southern Russian city of Krasnodar on Saturday, authorities said. The victim was attacked by a young man who randomly attacked people with a knife, police said.

item.title: Hundreds of swimmers lobby to end Berlin's ban on Spree River swimming
item.description: Several hundred people in Berlin took part in a swim-in protest on Saturday against the century-old ban on bathing in the city's Spree River. A spokesman for the Flussbad Berlin association said there were 500 swimmers and 500 people demonstrating on the banks.

item.title: Germany's The Left party elects co-leaders Pantisano and Schwerdtner
item.description: Germany's The Left Party on Saturday elected Luigi Pantisano alongside incumbent Ines Schwerdtner to lead the far-left party. At a party conference in Potsdam, Pantisano, a member of parliament, secured just over 53% despite running unopposed, a weak result.

item.title: Iran claims Strait of Hormuz closed in retaliation for US, Israel breaches of MoU
item.description: According to Mehr, Iran’s military declared the closure of the strait as the “first step” in its retaliation against what it deems violations of the US-Iran Memorandum of Understanding. Iran's top joint military command has ordered that the Strait of Hormuz be closed in retaliation for what it claims is a “blatant breach” of the recently signed US-Iran Memorandum of Understanding, according to Iranian semi-official state-sponsored media outlet Mehr on Saturday.

item.title: Bolivia declares state of emergency amid blockade crisis
item.description: For several weeks, a wave of protests has erupted over President Rodrigo Paz’s austerity measures.

item.title: Claude Guillemot, co-founder of Ubisoft, dies in plane crash
item.description: Claude Guillemot and a flight instructor were flying in a twin-motor Cessna 421 on Friday evening. An investigation into the crash is underway.

item.title: Iran says Strait of Hormuz will be closed over Israel attacks on Lebanon
item.description: Iran said Israel's continued attacks in Lebanon are a breach of Tehran's agreement with the US to end the war.

item.title: The Strait of Hormuz is closing again. Few ships were leaving the waterway in the first place
item.description: An initial wave of ships exiting the Strait of Hormuz after it officially reopened on Thursday seems to have slowed down.

item.title: Russia frees 24 Filipinos after Marcos talks with Putin
item.description: Russia has freed 24 Filipinos who have been detained for months without charges in a Siberian city, after Philippine President Ferdinand Marcos Jr. raised concern for them in a meeting with President Vladimir Putin, Philippine officials said Saturday. The 24 were scheduled to arrive in Manila in two flights early Sunday, with the first batch to be welcomed by Philippine Foreign Secretary Theresa Lazaro, who accompanied Marcos in his talks with Putin on Wednesday in the Russian city of Kazan, the Department of Foreign Affairs in Manila said.

item.title: Claude Guillemot, One Of Ubisoft's Co-Founders, has Died In A Plane Crash
item.description: The crash reportedly happened on an airstrip near the western coast of France on June 19.

item.title: Claude Guillemot, co-founder of Ubisoft, dies in plane crash
item.description: The chairman and CEO of Guillemot Corporation was 69.

item.title: Iran recloses Strait of Hormuz, citing Israeli strikes on Lebanon
item.description: It comes after Israel carried out a wave of fresh strikes on southern Lebanon on Saturday morning.

item.title: Mom of 2 Identified as Victim of Massive Fire That Broke Out at a Luxurious Beachside Hotel
item.description: “A vacation has turned into an unacceptable tragedy," Italy’s Tourism Minister Gianmarco Mazzi said, reacting to Francesca Valentino's death at the Viva Dominicus Beach Hotel in the Dominican Republic

item.title: Iran says it's closing the Strait of Hormuz again, blaming Israeli strikes on Lebanon — just days after Trump's deal reopened it
item.description: Iran said Saturday that it is reclosing the Strait of Hormuz, citing what Tehran called the U.S. failure to stop Israel's strikes on Lebanon. It is the first crisis since President Trump's memorandum of understanding with Iran was signed earlier this week.

item.title: One killed as freight train plunges from bridge in Germany after crash
item.description: Clean-up efforts continued in Munich on Saturday after two freight trains collided on a railway bridge overnight, killing one person and sending two wagons crashing onto a road below. One of the two wagons that was dangling from the bridge was lifted onto the road below by a heavy-duty crane around midday, police said.

item.title: Italian tourist killed in Dominican Republic beach resort fire
item.description: A fire at the Viva Wyndham Dominicus Beach Hotel led to the evacuation of nearly 1,700 tourists. One woman died and several were treated for injuries.

item.title: Services returning to normal at Edinburgh Airport after security alert
item.description: Services at Edinburgh Airport are returning to normal on Saturday after it was evacuated on Friday evening because of a security alert. An explosive ordnance disposal team was deployed to the airport to investigate what Police Scotland described as a “potentially suspicious package”, which was discovered at about 6:50pm (1750 GMT) on Friday.

item.title: Germany calls for compliance with ceasefire under Iran deal
item.description: Germany on Saturday called on all parties to implement the ceasefire agreed as part of the framework deal between the United States and Iran. Berlin is using its contacts, particularly with Israeli partners, to help ensure that the ceasefire agreement is put into practice, a spokesman for the German Foreign Ministry said.

item.title: Norway crown princess' son sentenced to four years in prison on two counts of rape
item.description: Marius Borg Høiby, 29, is the eldest son of Crown Princess Mette-Marit from a previous relationship and the stepson of the heir to the throne, Crown Prince Haakon.

item.title: Iran closes strait again because of Israel-Lebanon fighting
item.description: Iran said it has closed the Strait of Hormuz again as Israel and Lebanon continue fighting in spite of the peace plan between the U.S. and Iran.

item.title: Ukraine's Zelensky, top officials return Polish awards in WWII row
item.description: Ukrainian President Volodymyr Zelensky and his top officials said on Saturday they were returning Polish awards, in a spiralling row with key ally Poland over WWII massacres. Zelensky infuriated neighbouring Poland this month by naming a military unit after the nationalist Ukrainian Insurgent Army (UPA) that took part in massacres against Poles in WWII. On Friday, hard-right Polish President Karol Nawrocki stripped Zelensky of the Order of the White Eagle -- the highest honour in Poland -- despi

item.title: Russia reports drone attack on refinery in western Siberia
item.description: Drones have attacked an oil refinery in the western Siberian region of Tyumen, a senior Russian official said on Saturday. The facility was not damaged, according to the region's governor, Alexander Moor, who said workers were evacuated and emergency services were at the site of the resultant crash of debris.

item.title: Iran to close Strait of Hormuz due to Israeli attacks on Lebanon
item.description: Iran's military on Saturday said it will again close the Strait of Hormuz to all shipping, citing alleged Israeli violations of a ceasefire with Hezbollah in Lebanon that formed part of a recent framework deal between Tehran and Washington. In a statement reported in Iranian media, the military also pointed to Israel's continued presence in southern Lebanon and said shutting the key waterway for the global oil trade was "the first step" in responding to the "enemy's breach of trust.

item.title: Pakistan: US-Iran to begin technical talks in Switzerland on Sunday
item.description: Pakistan announced on Saturday that the technical-level talks between Iran and the United States to hammer out the complex operational details of their framework agreement will begin on Sunday. “As a follow-up to the signing of the Islamabad Memorandum of Understanding, technical-level talks will be held in Bürgenstock, Switzerland," on Sunday, Pakistan’s foreign ministry said on Saturday.

item.title: French police detain 20 at banned Iran opposition rally
item.description: French police arrested around 20 people in Paris on Saturday as demonstrators gathered for a protest against repression and executions in Iran, defying an official ban.The demonstrators were eventually dispersed, and a score of people were arrested, a police source told AFP. "They arrested about 20 people for no reason," Afchine Alavi, a member of the National Council of Resistance of Iran (NCRI), one Iranian opposition group, told AFP. "On the protesters' side, there is no violence.

item.title: 'Windrush event helps teach about merged history'
item.description: The Black Country Living Museum, in Dudley, held its annual Windrush event on Saturday.

item.title: Ubisoft Co-Founder Claude Guillemot Dead In Plane Crash In France
item.description: One of the founding members of Ubisoft, Claude Guillemot, died in a plane crash on Friday, June 19. He was 69.

item.title: Bolivia’s president declares a state of emergency as road blockades choke supplies
item.description: President Rodrigo Paz on Saturday declared a state of emergency that gives the military broad power to remove road blockades that have put a stranglehold on fuel and food supplies in Bolivia's seat of government and other major cities. A wave of protests over the last five weeks has called for Paz to step down over austerity measures imposed by the government, including the cancellation of fuel subsidies, and other issues.

item.title: Volcanic eruption injures sand miner in Indonesia
item.description: An eruption of Mount Semeru on Saturday left a sand miner with severe burns after he was struck by superheated volcanic material in Indonesia's East Java province, authorities and local media said. The eruption lasted about four minutes and generated pyroclastic flows, fast-moving avalanches of hot gas, ash and rock fragments from the volcano's summit, said Mukdas Sofian, a volcano observer at Indonesia's Centre for Volcanology and Geological Hazard Mitigation (PVMBG).

item.title: Hundreds turn out to counter Britain First demo
item.description: Police make arrests as Britain First and counter protesters gather in the city centre.

item.title: Third baby dies in Ebola orphanage outbreak as cases rapidly rise
item.description: The number of cases has increased by 38 percent over the past week and has spread to 32 health zones across eastern Congo.

item.title: Hackers suspected to be behind unauthorized alert sent to cell phones across Brazil
item.description: An unauthorized alert bearing a mysterious message that was sent to cell phones in several states across Brazil on Saturday morning is suspected to be the work of hackers, the Brazilian government said.

item.title: 1 dead, 9 injured in Dominican Republic resort fire
item.description: An Italian woman was killed, nine injured and more than 1,700 evacuated from a massive fire at a beachfront resort in the Dominican Republic Friday.

item.title: Iran declares Strait of Hormuz 'closed' over MOU 'breach'
item.description: Just moments later, U.S. Vice President JD Vance said on Fox News that “the straits really are open.”

item.title: A founder of Assassin's Creed maker Ubisoft killed in a plane crash in western France
item.description: A founder of global gaming company Ubisoft, maker of Assassin’s Creed, was killed in a plane crash in western France, authorities said Saturday. The twin-motor Cessna 421 carrying Claude Guillemot and a flight instructor crashed Friday evening near La Baule airport on the Atlantic coast, Mayor Franck Louvrier said in a statement. Ubisoft confirmed Guillemot's death but did not comment further.

item.title: Russia strikes an apartment building in the Ukrainian city of Kharkiv and 1 person is killed
item.description: Russian bombs struck an apartment building on Saturday in Kharkiv, Ukraine’s second-largest city, killing at least one person and wounding nine, including a 6-year-old child, authorities said. A body was pulled from the rubble hours after the attack, Mayor Ihor Terekhov said on Telegram. The head of the regional administration, Oleh Syniehubov, said that at least nine people were wounded, five of whom were hospitalized.

item.title: Spanish PM's wife must stand trial on corruption charges, judge rules
item.description: Source: Reuters

item.title: German MPs seek probe into development agency's Yemen operations
item.description: Lawmakers in Berlin are demanding an investigation into irregularities in the Yemen operations of the German government's GIZ development agency, which resulted in the dismissal of two dozen local staff members since 2023. "I take indications of a possible misappropriation of development aid funds very seriously," said Nicolas Zippelius, spokesman for the conservative CDU/CSU parliamentary group on development policy.

item.title: Zelensky's World War II dispute plays into Putin's hands
item.description: Poland has stripped Zelensky of their country's highest honor after he named a military unit after the Ukrainian Insurgent Army.

item.title: Man, 29, Went Missing as He Prepared to Hike a Historic Trail. More Than a Month Later, He Still Hasn't Been Found
item.description: Ian Treger was reported missing on May 13

item.title: Paris police arrest 20 as demonstrators defy ban on Iran opposition rally
item.description: PARIS, June 20 (Reuters) - Protestors against political executions in Iran gathered in Paris on Saturday in defiance of a ban on the rally, at which police arrested 20 people, according to organisers.

item.title: Iran Closes Strait of Hormuz, Shattering Fragile Ceasefire
item.description: Iran’s top joint military command announced the closure of the Strait of Hormuz to vessel traffic on June 20, 2026. The Khatam al-Anbiya Central Headquarters cited alleged U.S. and Israeli violations of the Islamabad Memorandum of Understanding signed just days earlier. The move directly challenges the recent ceasefire and threatens global energy flows with immediate

item.title: Crowds gather in Coleraine to celebrate Armed Forces Day
item.description: Events included an Armed Forces Day parade, a ceremonial gun salute and an RAF Falcons parachute display.

item.title: UK train collision leaves 28 in hospital, nine in critical condition
item.description: Twenty eight people remain in hospital, nine of them in a critical condition, after two trains collided near Bedford, police have said. The crash, which killed a train driver, involved two East Midlands Railway (EMR) services, one smashing into the back of the other on the same line shortly after 5 pm (1600 GMT) on Friday.

item.title: Israel Keeps Hammering Lebanon As Trump's Iran Deal Under Threat
item.description: Iranian and U.S. officials did not travel to Switzerland for weekend talks as planned.

item.title: Ubisoft cofounder Claude Guillemot dies in plane crash
item.description: "Ubisoft has learned with profound sadness of the death of Claude Guillemot," the video gaming giant told Newsweek.

item.title: Here’s The ‘Banned’ Trump Interview Sparking International Brawl — And The Bombshell Hit-Back
item.description: Trump sparked an international feud with a claim he made during a strange foreign interview, which drew a fiery rejoinder that lost little in translation.

item.title: Driver killed, 28 in hospital as UK train collision probed
item.description: UK accident investigators were on Saturday probing the cause of a train collision north of London which killed a driver and injured scores of passengers, nine critically.D'Orsi said that "specialist investigators from British Transport Police are working with colleagues at the Rail Accident Investigation Branch (RAIB) to gather the facts and determine what has happened".

item.title: UK condemns Israeli minister over inflammatory Lebanon remarks
item.description: UK foreign secretary slams Israeli minister’s inflammatory remarks amid regional tensions and fragile ceasefire.

item.title: Reports: Many killed in Israeli strikes on Lebanon despite ceasefire
item.description: Numerous Israeli airstrikes killed many people in Lebanon despite an agreed ceasefire, with the Lebanese civil defence authorities saying they had recovered 16 bodies in the southern city of Nabatiyeh alone, Lebanese media reported on Saturday. On Friday, US government sources said Israel and Lebanon's Iran-backed Hezbollah militia had agreed a new ceasefire after a major escalation in fighting between the two had threatened to derail a US-Iran deal to end the conflict in the region.

item.title: The Russian Navy has just shown its utter lack of seamanship
item.description: The Russian frigate Admiral Grigorovich firing warning shots across the bow of the British yacht Bright Future on June 16 was not an “isolated maritime misunderstanding” – no matter that our own Ministry of Defence would like you to think so. It illustrates two things: our own naval weakness, and the general incompetence of the Russian surface navy.

item.title: Two earthquakes shake the Greek holiday island Crete
item.description: Athens(dpa) - Two earthquakes measuring magnitudes of 4. 6 and 5.

item.title: 16 dead and 12 injured following Israeli strikes, Lebanon's civil defence agency says
item.description: It comes less than a day after US officials said Israel and the Iran-backed militant group Hezbollah had agreed to a renewed ceasefire.

item.title: India's Cockroach Party supporters bang plates to call for education minister's resignation
item.description: Supporters of India’s viral Cockroach Janta Party banged steel plates with spoons in a protest Saturday to demand the resignation of the education minister over allegations of examination irregularities and repeated paper leaks. “There is a virus called Dharmendra Pradhan that has to be removed,” he told the crowd, adding that the CJP was open to talks with the government if Pradhan stepped down.

item.title: Israeli fire kills five people in Gaza, including a child, medics say
item.description: CAIRO, June 20 (Reuters) - Israeli strikes and gunfire killed at least five people, including a child, in the Gaza Strip on Saturday, health officials said.

item.title: UK police probe cause of train collision that killed driver and left 9 in critical condition
item.description: Nine people were in critical condition on Saturday after a collision between two passenger trains in central England the night before killed the driver of one of the locomotives, police said. British Transport Police Chief Constable Lucy D'Orsi said more than 80 people were treated in hospitals after the crash late Friday afternoon, and 28 remained hospitalized a day later. Buckingham Palace said King Charles III “is greatly saddened” by the crash.

item.title: MSF says conditions in Lebanon’s Nabatieh resemble a ‘death trap’
item.description: Israel has stepped up attacks on parts of southern Lebanon, including Nabatieh, since midnight on Friday.

item.title: German journalist held in Syria back home, family says
item.description: Eva Maria Michelmann’s brother says she had been held in the Syrian city of Raqqa since January.

item.title: A freight trains' collision in Germany sends 2 wagons plunging off a bridge. One person was killed
item.description: Two freight trains collided on a railway bridge in Germany overnight, officials said Saturday, the crash sending two wagons plunging off the bridge and onto the street below. The wagons fell about 5 meters (16 feet) from the bridge, dpa said. Munich police said the street beneath the bridge has been closed and urged drivers to avoid the area while recovery and clearing operations were underway.

item.title: Tourist Dead, Nearly 1,700 Others Evacuated After Fire Engulfs Luxurious Beachside Hotel
item.description: The deadly blaze occurred at a luxury beach resort in the Dominican Republic on Friday, June 19

item.title: Surfer Escapes Death After Shark Bit Him and Dragged Him Underwater: ‘I Got Off Lightly’
item.description: Surfer Alejo Santiñaque escaped from the jaws of death in Australia by fighting back against a shark that sank its teeth into his foot and pulled him beneath the water. Uruguayan national Alejo, who’s lived Down Under for nearly two years, revealed on Facebook he was sitting on his board waiting for a wave at […]

item.title: Pakistani interior minister in Iran for talks on finalizing peace deal
item.description: Pakistani Interior Minister Mohsin Naqvi arrived in Iran on Saturday to discuss the continuation of negotiations with the United States in Switzerland with the Iranian leadership. According to the pro-government Iranian news agency Mehr, Naqvi's plane first landed in the pilgrimage city of Mashhad in the country's north-east.

item.title: Why is there fighting in Lebanon and does it threaten the Iran deal?
item.description: Persistent fighting between Israeli forces and Hezbollah in southern Lebanon is threatening a tentative deal reached between the United States and Iran to end their war.

item.title: Deadly fire destroys luxury Caribbean resort
item.description: A huge fire has ripped through a luxury beach resort in the Caribbean, killing an Italian tourist and forcing nearly 1,700 holidaymakers to evacuate.

item.title: An Israeli strike hits a Gaza City apartment, killing 2 children, Palestinian health officials say
item.description: DEIR AL-BALAH, Gaza (AP) — An Israeli strike early Saturday killed at least two children in the Gaza Strip, Palestinian health officials said. Despite an October ceasefire between Israel and the militant group Hamas, the enclave has seen near-daily Israeli attacks that have killed over 1,007 Palestinians, the Gaza Health Ministry said. Saturday's strike hit an apartment in Gaza City around 2 a.m local time, according to the ministry, with the death toll expected to rise as rescue teams work on recovering more bodies.

item.title: Taiwan President Pushes Back On China Pressure, Seeks Swift Approval Of US Arms Package
item.description: Taiwan President Lai Ching-te said Taiwan’s efforts to strengthen its defenses should not be viewed as provocation, as Beijing intensifies military and political pressure and Taipei seeks swift approval of new U.S. arms sales. Taiwan Pushes Back On China On Thursday, speaking to the Taiwan Foreign Correspondents’ Club in Taipei, Lai said Taiwan’s actions are focused on protecting its democratic system and security, reported Reuters. "Taiwan’s safeguarding of its own national security and maintai

item.title: The Real Love Company made her feel whole. Then ‘Daddy’ said to strip naked.
item.description: Greg Baer, the founder of the Real Love Company, told his followers to call him Daddy and held them in his lap as if they were babies.

item.title: Man charged with attempted murder, released after allegedly forcing toddler into crocodile enclosure at zoo
item.description: A man was charged with attempted murder after allegedly forcing a 3-year-old boy into a crocodile enclosure at a zoo in Huntingdon, England.

item.title: Americans are dying fighting for Ukraine. Here’s why
item.description: It’s estimated at least 118 Americans have died fighting in Ukraine after volunteering for the war effort.

item.title: Dominican Republic Resort Fire Kills Tourist, Evacuates 1,690 Guests
item.description: A fire broke out at a resort in the Dominican Republic, claiming the life of one tourist and triggering a large-scale evacuation. Emergency responders moved quickly to clear the property, relocating roughly 1,690 guests to nearby hotels and other facilities. The incident has drawn renewed attention to safety preparedness at popular Caribbean destinations. Details of

item.title: China is counting its wins from the Iran war
item.description: When US and Israeli bombs first began falling on Iran at the end of February, China’s leaders were staring at the very real possibility of another friendly regime being decapitated, much like had happened with Venezuela only weeks before.

item.title: One dead and several wounded in Russian attack on Ukraine's Kharkiv
item.description: At least one person has been killed in a Russian air attack on the eastern Ukrainian city of Kharkiv, Governor Oleh Synyehubov said on Saturday. Rescue workers were recovering the person's body from the rubble after a glide bomb struck a two-storey apartment building, he posted on Telegram.

item.title: Report: Many killed in Israeli airstrikes in Lebanon despite ceasefire
item.description: Lebanese media on Saturday reported numerous Israeli airstrikes that killed many people despite an agreed ceasefire, with the Lebanese civil defence authorities saying they had recovered 16 bodies in the city of Nabatiyeh alone. On Friday, US government sources said Israel and Lebanon's Iranian-backed Hezbollah militia had agreed a new ceasefire after a major escalation in fighting between both had threatened to derail a US-Iran deal to end the conflict in the region.

item.title: Moscow oil refinery attack brings Russia's war with Ukraine closer to home
item.description: There are moments when life in Moscow feels completely normal - Thursday morning wasn't one of them, writes Steve Rosenberg.

item.title: Israel strikes south Lebanon despite truce announced with Hezbollah
item.description: Israel carried out deadly strikes in south Lebanon on Saturday and Hezbollah maintained it had the right to respond, hours after the United States announced a renewed ceasefire in fighting that had strained a fledgling deal with Iran.Hezbollah has not officially claimed any attacks on Israel or its troops in Lebanon since the ceasefire was announced.

item.title: 'Most wanted' suspect arrested in Belgium
item.description: Drugs and a firearm were recovered when Spencer Dillon Lamb was detained by Belgian police officers.

item.title: A top banker made a case for mining to Pope Leo XIV, who has seen its impact up close
item.description: The head of Latin America’s top development bank made a pitch to Pope Leo XIV this week in the face of the Vatican’s call to divest from the mining industry: that the mistakes of the past can be avoided in extracting rare earth minerals to supply a global tech boom. Ilan Goldfajn, head of the Inter-American Development Bank, met privately with the pope on Friday and asserted the potential of rare earth mining, saying it could be a boon to Latin America provided there are safeguards and value is added locally.

item.title: One killed as freight train plunges from bridge in Germany after crash
item.description: One person died after two freight trains collided on a bridge in Munich overnight, causing loaded cars to plunge to the road below, police said on Saturday. Two cars of one train fell about 5 metres from a bridge and came to rest at round 1:40 am (2340 GMT Friday), according to a spokeswoman.

item.title: Paradise inferno as fire engulfs Dominican resort, kills tourist
item.description: About 1,690 guests were evacuated and relocated to nearby hotels and other facilities, according to emergency services.

item.title: Ukrainian officials criticize Polish president's decision to strip Zelenskyy of honor
item.description: A decision by Polish President Karol Nawrocki to strip Ukrainian leader Volodymyr Zelenskyy of Poland’s highest state honor was beneficial to Moscow, which has an interest in seeing Kyiv in conflict with its allies, Ukrainian officials said. Nawrocki announced on Friday that he will strip Zelenskyy of the Order of the White Eagle over the Ukrainian leader’s decision to name a military unit after a Ukrainian paramilitary organization accused of massacring Poles during World War II.

item.title: Fighting persists in Lebanon despite a ceasefire as the US-Iran deal is under threat
item.description: The persistent fighting threatened an interim agreement between the United States and Iran to end the war in the Middle East. Lebanon’s National News Agency said the strikes hit the southern town of Nabatiyeh and nearby villages. Mediators were scrambling to halt the fighting between Israel and the militant Lebanese Hezbollah group, after a heavy exchange on Friday killed at least 47 people in Lebanon and four Israeli soldiers.

item.title: Man finds family heirloom—then experts confirm what it is
item.description: “What I'd dismissed as common was actually a fragile survivor of a vanishing written culture," the 28-year-old told Newsweek.

item.title: Several reported killed in Israeli strikes on Lebanon despite ceasefire
item.description: The IDF said it struck "Hezbollah terrorist targets" after the group fired over 50 projectiles at Israeli forces in southern Lebanon.

item.title: Pope Leo XIV heads to northern Italy to honor St. Augustine and migrant saint Cabrini
item.description: Pope Leo XIV is traveling Saturday to northern Italy to pay homage to two figures of Christian devotion: St. Augustine, the inspiration for his religious order, and Mother Frances Cabrini, the patron of migrants who became the first American saint. The pope's first stop Saturday is the Lombardy city of Pavia to pray at the tomb of St. Augustine, the fifth-century titan of early Christianity who centuries later inspired the formation of Leo's Augustinian religious order.

item.title: 'When am I going home?' Palestinian doctor detained in Israel for nearly 16 months
item.description: Dr. Hussam Abu Safiya was arrested in December 2024.

item.title: Britain’s biggest data centre to be powered by gas
item.description: Britain’s largest data centre will be powered by gas because of delays connecting to the grid.

item.title: Two roadside bombs kill at least seven in northwestern Pakistan
item.description: No group has yet claimed responsibility for the attacks.

item.title: Head of Zelensky's office refuses Polish honour
item.description: The head of the Ukrainian presidential office, Kyrylo Budanov, has said he will follow his country's top diplomat in returning a Polish state award amid a historical dispute between the neighbouring allies against Russia. Budanov wrote on Telegram on Saturday that he is renouncing the Golden Officer's Cross of the Order of Merit of the Republic of Poland, which he received last year.

item.title: Man, 28, Died in Car Accident After Leaving Best Friend’s Wedding to ‘Get a Phone Charger’
item.description: “My brother’s car ... was smashed from both sides so there was no chance of survival,” said Vlad Merkulovs, the brother of Daniel Rjabcevs

item.title: At least seven people killed in Pakistan blast

Nearly two weeks after his return to his homeland, James, 52, is trying to settle back into the life he left 10 years ago when he packed his suitcases and headed for South Africa on a six-month visitor’s visa. The attack on James came in the midst of a sharp rise in anti-migrant sentiment in South Africa in recent months, when there have been marches calling for immigrants in the country illegally to leave and reports of violence against some foreign nationals.

As Britain struggles to build a modern fighting force against a backdrop of constrained resources and competing priorities, it is vital that we learn the right lessons from Ukraine. Equally importantly, however, we must not discard the hard-learnt lessons of the last century, from the Somme and Passchendaele to the Donbas front line.

item.title: Photos show Lebanon's shattered south as towns and villages await peace
item.description: On Friday, officials said Israel and Hezbollah agreed to stop attacking each other, raising fresh hopes that the conflict could ease after repeated ceasefires failed to hold. In the coastal city of Tyre, after a U.S.-Iran preliminary deal to end the war in the region was signed, residents returned to neighborhoods reduced to rubble by Israeli strikes. Apartment buildings stand gutted, streets are lined with debris, and families are beginning to assess the damage left behind.

BTP said their officers responded to reports of the crash at around 5:15 pm (1615 GMT) on Friday.

item.title: Tyre in southern Lebanon marks Muharram holy month after destruction from Israel-Hezbollah war
item.description: Wearing a yellow scarf showing her son killed fighting for Hezbollah in southern Lebanon, Iman Dilbani wept during a religious sermon Friday in the battered southern city of Tyre during the Islamic holy month of Muharram. Tyre, Lebanon’s fourth largest city, has been devastated by the Israel-Hezbollah war that reached a reported ceasefire Friday, with damaged buildings and structures reduced to rubble seen on almost every street following intense Israeli airstrikes. Muharram is among the holiest months for Shiite Muslims and marks the martyrdom of the Prophet Muhammad’s grandson, Imam Hussein, and his 72 companions in the battle of Karbala in the seventh century in present-day Iraq.

item.description: African officials must step up financing to respond and develop vaccines for the Ebola outbreak in Congo and Uganda, the continent's top health agency said on Friday, warning that the continent cannot continue to rely on foreign partners for its health needs. The outbreak has claimed more than 200 lives out of 894 confirmed cases since May 15, with up to 35,000 contacts, the Africa Centers for Disease Control and Prevention said.

item.title: Two trains collide north of London, killing at least one person
item.description: Emergency services rushed to the scene of a collision between two trains north of London on Friday afternoon that killed at least one person. A passenger reported that he was thrown forward by the impact then saw fellow travelers with broken bones and bloody injuries. Both trains were traveling south to London St. Pancras station when they collided outside the town of Bedford around 5:15 p.m., according to information on rail tracking websites.

item.title: Ghana conference urges slave-trade nations to issue apologies and reparations
item.description: African and Caribbean leaders in Ghana on Friday urged former slave-trading nations to issue apologies and reparations over the trafficking of enslaved Africans after a landmark U.N. resolution in March declaring it “the gravest crime against humanity.” The “Next Steps” conference in the Ghanaian capital of Accra issued a declaration calling on countries involved in the Atlantic slave trade to “offer full, formal and unconditional apologies as a foundational step towards reconciliation, trust-building and reparatory justice.”

item.title: Kim Gamel, a former veteran AP correspondent in Europe and the Middle East, dies
item.description: Kim Gamel, a former veteran Associated Press correspondent who covered conflicts and uprisings around the Middle East, including the height of the Iraq war and the Arab Spring, has died, according to her brother-in-law. Gamel died Wednesday in Idaho after a yearslong battle with lymphoma, Lee Ruff said. Gamel brought the same care and compassion to her work reporting and directing coverage in war zones that she offered to her colleagues.

item.title: Mourners bury a 6-month-old Ebola victim, marking third orphanage death as Congo outbreak spreads
item.description: Mourners gathered Friday to bury a 6-month-old girl who died from Ebola earlier this week, the third child to die at an orphanage in eastern Congo as authorities have struggled to contain the latest outbreak. Carrying a cross, people stood at a distance as the small coffin was lowered into the ground by masked and gloved health workers, and a Catholic priest prayed over her body. Ituri, the region at the center of the current outbreak, has reported more than 90% of the cases.

item.title: Police probe attack on German Interior Ministry official's car
item.description: Suspected left-wing extremists have carried out a butyric acid attack in Hamburg on the private car of Christoph de Vries, Parliamentary State Secretary at the Federal Ministry of the Interior. On Thursday morning, a car owner noticed a strong, foul odour coming from his car at his home address and reported it to the police later that day, a police spokeswoman said, without mentioning de Vries’ name.

item.description: A boat carrying dozens of migrant s bound for European shores capsized off the coast of Libya last week, leaving 51 people dead or missing, a monitoring group said on Friday. Ten migrants survived the shipwreck, which occurred on June 12 in the Mediterranean Sea, off the eastern part of the North African nation, according to the Abreen group, which tracks movements of migrants in eastern Libya. It was the latest tragedy in this part of the Mediterranean Sea.

Prosecutors said she was forced out of a car at gunpoint, with three people now arrested and her husband and brother-in-law on the run. No warrant had been issued for her arrest at the time of writing.

The opposition Strong Armenia party appealed to the Constitutional Court to invalidate election results or call a second round of voting, and denounced the arrests of opposition politicians as an assault on democracy.

item.description: As a barrage of Ukrainian drones descended on Moscow in the early hours of Thursday, Russia’s response appeared more like a scramble, and less like a well-planned strategic defense. Videos from the streets of the capital showed glimpses of the ensuing chaos.

item.title: Fatalities caused by Russian shelling in Kramatorsk
item.description: At least two people have been killed by Russian shelling in the eastern Ukrainian city of Kramatorsk. Six other people were injured, according to Vadym Filashkin, the military governor of the Donetsk region, who posted the news on Telegram on Friday.

item.description: No spring, no river, no lake: "Every litre counts" at French mountain lodges where water is scarce but visitor numbers are booming."Every litre counts, of course.

item.title: Canadian auto parts magnate Frank Stronach found guilty of sexual assault
item.description: Austrian-Canadian billionaire and automotive business founder Frank Stronach was found guilty Friday of sexual assault and indecent assault of two woman decades ago. Stronach, who is 93, had been accused of alleged incidents involving seven complainants, and pleaded not guilty to 12 charges. Ontario Superior Court Justice Anne Molloy found Stronach guilty on Friday on two charges related to two complainants.

item.title: Germany's IKEA stores affected as workers go on strike
item.description: Thousands of retail workers across Germany have once again walked out in the current round of collective bargaining. The focus of Friday's action, called by the Verdi trade union, was the furniture retailer IKEA.

item.title: Brazen attack on Niger’s airport shows jihadis are expanding to cities in Africa's Sahel
item.description: The gunfire and explosions that ripped through Niger’s main international airport are the latest sign of armed groups increasingly targeting cities and urban centers in Africa’s Sahel region, where they are competing for influence and territories, analysts say. The al-Qaeda-linked JNIM jihadi group, the most potent in the Sahel region south of the Sahara desert, claimed responsibility for Thursday's attack at Diori Hamani International Airport in Niamey that killed 11 soldiers and two civilians. It's also the headquarters of the regional alliance that bring together troops from Niger, Mali and Burkina Faso.

item.title: Hundreds of nurses given safety alarms after racist violence
item.description: The alarms were distributed to international nurses based at Altnagelvin Hospital in Londonderry.

item.title: Israel, Hezbollah agree truce, US says, as fighting derails Iran talks
item.description: Israel and the Iranian-backed Hezbollah militia in Lebanon have agreed a new ceasefire, US government sources said on Friday, after a major escalation in fighting threatened to derail diplomatic efforts to end the conflict in the region. The agreement, brokered by the United States and Qatar, came into effect at 1300 GMT, the sources said.

item.title: ‘Hideous’: Left and Right Slam Israeli Minister Declaring ‘All of Lebanon Must Burn’
item.description: Jake Tapper replied, “Ben Gvir, an anti-Arab racist zealot, says ‘all of Lebanon must burn.’ That’s almost 6 million people. Hideous.”

item.title: Photos show Nagas protesting against rising cases of sexual violence
item.description: Thousand of people from the Naga community marched in pouring rain on Friday to demand justice for victims of sexual violence after a spate of cases reported in the state. In Kohima, the capital of Nagaland, the protesters said it was time to break the silence around sexual violence for fear of societal recrimination and discrimination. The small mountain state, that has consistently ranked as one of the safest places for women in India, has been shaken by a chain of sexual assaults in the past few months.

item.title: Fisherman Missing at Sea for 7 Days Found Alive Wearing T-Shirt and Shorts in Tiny 13-Foot Boat
item.description: The man was found on Friday, June 19, in an aluminum rowboat in the northern Cook Islands, which is around 2,100 miles north of New Zealand

item.title: Ukraine is putting weapons stations on ground robots to make 'small tanks' that hunt Russia's infiltration teams
item.description: Ukraine's Frontline Robotics makes a remote weapons station that used to be stationary but can now be put on a robot to make a "small tank."

item.title: Israel and Hezbollah Agree to Cease-Fire, U.S. Official Says
item.description: The development comes after the U.S.-Iran peace talks set to take place in Switzerland on Friday were abruptly postponed.

item.title: Norway imposes near ban on AI in elementary school
item.description: Norway is imposing a near ban on the use of generative AI tools by elementary school pupils while ‌also restricting their use in the education of older children to ‌prevent a negative impact on learning, the country's prime minister said on Friday. Facing a broad ​decline in education test scores, the government in 2024 banned smartphones from schools and has given teachers back more powers to enforce discipline in the classroom. Using AI increases the risk that young children skip important steps in their education, ‌Prime Minister Jonas Gahr Stoere ⁠told a press conference on Friday.

item.description: Local air defences shot down 79 of 90 Russian drones from Thursday evening into Friday morning, per Ukraine's air force.

item.title: Six-year-old Ebola patient taken from DR Congo hospital found and 'doing well'
item.description: Health facilities have come under attack during the current outbreak as a result of misinformation and fear.

item.title: Animal rights activists: Icelandic whaling resumes
item.description: Icelandic whalers are resuming their controversial hunt for fin whales after a two-year pause, animal welfare campaigners said on Friday. Two Icelandic whaling vessels left the port in the capital, Reykjavík, Pro Wildlife and other animal welfare organizations said.

item.title: Argentina asks US to block 13K parents from World Cup matches
item.description: The individuals are accused of failing to make child support payments.

item.description: The Taliban is smashing smartphones after banning civil servants and soldiers from using them.

item.title: At least 30 deaths at Congo camp show Ebola could be spreading fast
item.description: BUNIA, Democratic Republic of Congo, June 19 (Reuters) - At least 30 people have died since the start of May in one camp for displaced civilians in northeastern Congo, a death rate that camp officials said was unprecedented, and, because of the symptoms, could indicate Ebola is ‌spreading fast there. It was not possible to confirm the causes of death because patients or their relatives in Kigonze camp in Bunia - the epicentre of the Ebola outbreak in ‌Democratic Republic of Congo - had until Th

item.title: Zelensky: Ukraine effectively has 'NATO's second-strongest army'
item.description: Ukrainian President Volodymyr Zelensky has described his country's armed forces as effectively "NATO's second-strongest army. " Zelensky told journalists that "NATO needs us," the Hromadske website reported on Friday.

item.title: Podcast: Why vascular care in England needs reformation
item.description: A recent vascular care reform whitepaper for England highlighted gaps in care and issues that cause avoidable harm to patients.

item.title: Turkey bars public World Cup screening over university entrance exam
item.description: Turkey's interior ministry has instructed governors nationwide not to permit the installation of giant public screens for the national team's World Cup match against Paraguay, citing the need to minimise noise and traffic disruption during the country's university entrance examinations. The YKS is Turkey's nationwide university entrance examination and is taken annually by hundreds of thousands of students seeking admission to higher education institutions.

item.title: BREAKING: Israel and Hezbollah Agree to Ceasefire
item.description: The ceasefire renewal comes after 18 people were killed in Lebanon following overnight Israeli air strikes, despite the U.S.-Iran MOU.

item.title: Venice may charge people up to €50 to visit the city
item.description: Venice may charge significantly higher entrance fees for tourists under a proposal by the city's new mayor, as popular places around the world seek ways to cap the number of visitors in peak season. "If it currently stands at between €5 and €10 [$5.

item.title: Bravery in Burma: Despite being rendered blind by the Japanese, this soldier fought on
item.description: Marching 200 miles inside northern Burma and determined to cut off the enemy, 1st Lt. Jack L. Knight made the ultimate sacrifice.

item.title: Israel and Hezbollah agree to renew their ceasefire, officials say
item.description: Fighting in southern Lebanon had postponed Iran-U.S. talks in Switzerland, according to officials.

item.title: Israel and Hezbollah agree new ceasefire, US officials say
item.description: Israel and the Iranian-backed Hezbollah militia in Lebanon have agreed a new ceasefire, US government sources said on Friday. The agreement, brokered by the United States and Qatar, came into effect at 4 pm (1300 GMT), the sources said.

item.title: The prolific shoplifters making a mockery of Britain’s laws
item.description: Far from a faceless army of opportunists stalking our streets, a small number of prolific offenders are driving Britain’s shoplifting crisis, according to new figures. Matt Twist, the Metropolitan Police’s assistant commissioner, says just a few repeat thieves are responsible for thousands of crimes.

item.title: Iran’s hardliners threaten to spoil the regime’s victory lap
item.description: Iranian officials and state media have celebrated it as a triumph for the Islamic Republic and a defeat for the United States. But not everyone in Iran sees it that way, and many of the deal’s critics are both influential and well-connected.

item.title: Refugee camp deaths raise fears of new disease outbreak in the Congo
item.description: At least 10 people have died in a refugee camp near the centre of the Congo’s Ebola epidemic, raising fears of a new disease outbreak.

item.title: Ebola spreading 'fast' in DR Congo, warns WHO
item.description: The fatal Ebola outbreak in the Democratic Republic of Congo is spreading rapidly, the World Health Organization warned Friday, despite accelerating efforts to tackle the virus.The outbreak has also spread to North Kivu and South Kivu provinces.

item.title: Attempted murder charge after 'hate crime' attack
item.description: A 17-year-old is charged and appears in court while another 17-year-old is arrested, police say.

item.title: UK law enforcement destroyed my reputation and integrity, ex-Nigerian oil minister tells BBC
item.description: Diezani Alison-Madueke was cleared of taking bribes from wealthy oil executives after six-month trial.

item.title: Trapped on Everest for days, Nepali survivor recounts escape
item.description: For three days, Nepali mountaineer Dawa Sherpa was trapped deep inside an Everest crevasse, surviving on biscuits, chocolates and chunks of ice, while back home his family had already begun mourning his death.But he was still deep inside the crevasse.

item.title: Replica Columbus ship arrives in Eastbourne
item.description: The Nao Santa Maria arrives at Sovereign Harbour where the public can come on board.

item.title: Spanish king and crown princess on joint training flight
item.description: Spain’s Crown Princess Leonor, 20, and her father, King Felipe VI, 58, have completed their first joint training flight in Spanish Air Force aircraft. Each flew a two-seater Pilatus PC-21 turboprop aircraft from the airbase of the Spanish Air Force Officers’ Academy over the Mediterranean, according to a statement published online by the Royal Household on Friday.

item.title: ICC prosecutor temporary suspended from British bar
item.description: The British bar association temporarily suspended the embattled chief prosecutor of the International Criminal Court on Friday, pending the outcome of disciplinary proceedings. British barrister Karim Khan was formally removed from his duties at the ICC last week after the court's oversight body found he had engaged in “serious misconduct” over accusations he had an inappropriate relationship with a female staff member. The move by the Bar Standards Board, which regulates lawyers practicing in England and Wales, is the latest challenge Khan is facing.

item.title: France's Thales teams up with Hanwha on long-range strike systems
item.description: French technology group Thales and South Korea's Hanwha Aerospace have agreed to cooperate on land-based long-range precision strike systems. The French firm announced on Friday that the two companies have signed a memorandum of understanding to this effect.

item.title: Stepmum jailed for 1978 killing of girl, 5
item.description: Andrea suffered severe burns to half her body and died in hospital on 13 July 1978.

item.title: A conflict over cattle in Brazil's Amazon highlights tensions for Indigenous peoples
item.description: On a vast island in northern Brazil, an unusual debate about cattle and conservation is taking place. Federal authorities last year ordered the removal of herds from protected Indigenous territory on the world's largest river island, Bananal Island. The events underscore the challenge of balancing conservation, Indigenous interests and pressure from agribusiness, one of Brazil’s most powerful sectors.

item.title: G-7 Vows Crackdown on North Korean Crypto Theft Funding Nuclear Arms
item.description: Group of Seven leaders pledged a coordinated crackdown on North Korea’s multi-billion dollar cryptocurrency t...

item.title: Russia attacks merchant ships in Black Sea, killing crew member
item.description: The Russian military has attacked two civilian merchant ships in the Black Sea with drones, Ukrainian authorities reported on Friday. Deputy Prime Minister Oleksii Kuleba and the port administration said a crew member on a Panamanian-flagged vessel was killed in the attack, while two other seafarers were injured, one of them seriously.

item.title: Fresh Israeli strikes trigger new exodus in southern Lebanon
item.description: Renewed Israeli airstrikes on southern Lebanon have prompted hundreds of residents to flee their homes once again, underscoring the fragility of a US-Iran framework agreement that was intended to end regional violence. Scores of cars packed with families were seen heading north from southern Lebanon toward the port city of Sidon on Friday, seeking shelter from the escalating fighting.

item.title: Israel strikes southern Lebanon as fighting strains peace efforts
item.description: The Israeli military launched fresh airstrikes on southern Lebanon on Friday, as renewed fighting with the Hezbollah militia threatened to derail diplomatic efforts to end the Iran war. Airstrikes were carried out overnight and on Friday morning on positions of the Shiite Hezbollah militia in southern Lebanon, the Israel Defense Forces (IDF) said.

item.title: Seven men plead not guilty to child rape
item.description: They deny 38 offences, including rape and sex trafficking.

item.title: IDF strikes 80 Hezbollah targets, kills dozens of terrorists after four soldiers killed
item.description: After four Israeli soldiers were killed, the IDF also announced that they had attacked two Hezbollah command centers in the Beqaa Valley, while terrorists were inside. The IDF struck over 80 Hezbollah targets across Lebanon overnight in response to repeated violations of the ceasefire by the terrorist group, the military announced on Friday.

item.title: Hundreds of Thousands of Mice Are Swarming Australia’s Farms
item.description: A mouse plague of Biblical proportions has struck parts of Australia, and it’s not for the first time. The startling scenes in this Instagram post show hundreds of thousands of mice streaming around agricultural equipment and across roads. Farmers and the authorities can only do their best against a relentless onslaught. Australia’s Worst Mouse Plague

item.title: WHO reports 75 health-care workers infected with Ebola
item.description: The World Health Organization (WHO) has reported that 75 health-care workers have been infected with the dangerous Ebola virus during the outbreak in the Democratic Republic of the Congo and Uganda. Seventeen of them have died, Marie-Roseline Belizaire, the WHO's representative in Bunia, told reporters in Geneva on Friday.

item.title: EU leaders squabble over outreach to Moscow as Ukraine war rages on
item.description: European Union leaders have been unable to agree on setting up a back-channel with Moscow to ensure that the bloc’s interests are protected should progress be made in negotiations to end Russia’s war on Ukraine, some of them said on Friday. European Council President António Costa, who chaired their two-day summit, had directed his office to reach out to the Kremlin and proposed a senior official to make contact. Costa said his aim was not to mediate or set up a parallel negotiating track to the one led by the United States, which is making little progress.

item.title: Iranian Singer Parastoo Ahmadi Reportedly Sentenced to 74 Lashes Over Hijab-Free YouTube Concert
item.description: Iranian singer Parastoo Ahmadi has reportedly been sentenced to 74 lashes after performing without a hijab in a livestreamed YouTube concert. The Guardian reported that ... Read More

item.title: Stepmum guilty of 1978 scalding killing of girl, 5
item.description: Janice Nix punished Andrea Bernard by forcing her to take a scalding bath at their south London home.

item.title: St James's Park pelicans hatch first chicks
item.description: Four chicks hatch at the central London park, where pelicans were first introduced in 1664.

item.title: Kenya's Ruto says country nearing US mineral deal
item.description: Kenya has large, untapped deposits of minerals required to build infrastructure for renewable energy and digital infrastructure.

item.title: New Russian strikes hit Kharkiv and Odesa, killing one and injuring children
item.description: New Russian strikes hit Ukraine's Kharkiv and Odesa regions on 19 June, killing one person, injuring children and adults, and damaging homes, vehicles and fuel facilities overnight.

item.title: Afghanistan launches 'airstrikes' on Pakistan
item.description: Afghanistan says it has attacked several targets inside Pakistan by air in the latest flare-up in the simmering conflict between the neighbouring countries. The Afghan Ministry of Defence wrote on X on Friday that it had carried out "airstrikes" at various locations inside Pakistan.

item.title: Armenia sees arrests and calls for election rerun
item.description: Pro-Russian politicians in Armenia have called for a rerun of the country's parliamentary election following the arrest of several opposition figures in the South Caucasus republic. The Strong Armenia bloc, led by billionaire Samvel Karapetyan, has filed a petition with the Constitutional Court seeking the annulment of the election results, local media reported on Friday.

item.title: 2 men sentenced to prison for arson on property linked to UK Prime Minister Keir Starmer
item.description: A British court sent two men to prison Friday for setting fire to property linked to British Prime Minister Keir Starmer in a plot orchestrated by a mysterious Russian-speaking figure. Ukrainian national Roman Lavrynovych, 22, and Stanislav Carpiuc, a 27-year-old Romanian citizen, were imprisoned for 7 years and 2 years, respectively, after being found guilty of conspiracy to damage property by fire. The men targeted a car and two properties linked to Starmer over three nights in May 2025 on the orders of a Russian-speaking figure going by the name of “El Money,” according to prosecutors.

item.title: Afghanistan strikes targets in Pakistan, raising cross-border tension
item.description: The strikes are the latest threat to the fragile ceasefire between Afghanistan and Pakistan.

item.title: Ukraine's small drone teams keep beating NATO units in exercises, and neither side is surprised
item.description: Ukraine has more battlefield drone experience than many NATO allies, and it's eager to share its hard-won expertise.

item.title: Brezhnev’s great-grandson ‘captured by Ukraine’
item.description: The great-grandson of Leonid Brezhnev, the Soviet leader, was captured while fighting in Ukraine, Russian media has claimed.

item.title: More than 70 Congo medics infected with Ebola since outbreak started, WHO says
item.description: NAIROBI, June 19 (Reuters) - A senior World Health Organization official said on Friday that 75 ‌medics in the Democratic Republic of Congo had ‌been infected with Ebola and 17 of them had died since the ​current outbreak started there. Ebola was thought to be circulating months before the outbreak was first declared by Congolese officials on May 15, meaning many medics were exposed to the disease ‌before they even ⁠knew it was present.

item.title: Japan joins the global craze to field interceptor drones
item.description: The interceptor drone systems are expected to be installed by 2027 near radar sites, bases, vessels and other critical locations, the agency stated.

item.title: UNICEF: Ceasefire in Gaza a 'deadly illusion' for children
item.description: The ceasefire in the Gaza Strip announced in October is “a deadly illusion” for children and young people, the United Nations Children’s Fund (UNICEF) said on Friday. UNICEF spokesman James Elder reported that 265 minors have been killed since then, more than 90% of them as a result of Israeli attacks.

item.title: Survey: Majority in favour of German Strait of Hormuz deployment
item.description: Most people in Germany would welcome the country's armed forces taking part in an operation to secure the Strait of Hormuz, if the ceasefire in Iran holds. A survey conducted for public broadcaster ZDF found that 57% would welcome Germany joining other European countries in such an operation, while 38% were not in favour.

item.description: “That one was tricky. These snakes can get into the most difficult places to retrieve them from."

item.description: Iran's next generation is taking over.

item.title: Gaza ceasefire a 'deadly illusion': UNICEF
item.description: The ceasefire declared in Gaza more than eight months ago is a "deadly illusion", the UN charged on Friday, with 265 children killed there since the fighting was supposed to have stopped.Yet for Palestinian children, this so-called ceasefire has become a cruel and a deadly illusion."

item.title: Mayor accused of faking her kidnapping in embezzlement scheme
item.description: Nancy Napoles denied the accusation and said she was willing to cooperate with authorities to clarify what happened.

item.title: Netanyahu: IDF will exact 'very heavy price' from Hezbollah after deaths of four soldiers
item.description: "I told the Prime Minister, even in our private meetings: For every tear of an Israeli mother, a thousand Lebanese mothers must weep," Ben-Gvir wrote on X/Twitter. Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu instructed the IDF to strike at Hezbollah "with full force" in response to four IDF soldiers being killed by Hezbollah on Friday.

item.title: Boy, 12, wins hearts after trying to check sick chicken into Ethiopian hospital
item.description: Markos's beloved hen was not responding to any treatments - so he did the only thing he could think of.

item.title: Former member of Regensburg boys' choir loses abuse damages case
item.description: A former member of Germany's renowned Regensburger Domspatzen (Cathedral Sparrows) boys' choir has lost a lawsuit seeking damages from the Catholic diocese of Regensburg over alleged abuse he says he suffered as a child. The Regensburg Regional Court dismissed the claim on the grounds that it was time-barred under Germany's statute of limitations, a court spokesman said on Friday.

item.title: Lebanon air raids kill 18 as residents flee southern villages
item.description: Lebanon reported 18 deaths on 19 June after Israeli airstrikes hit several locations in the country's south and east, raising fresh concerns about the durability of a regional peace initiative announced earlier this week.

item.description: Edwards has been the boss of the data watchdog since January 2022, and said he had made inappropriate attempts at humour.

item.title: Four IDF soldiers killed while fighting in southern Lebanon, military announces
item.description: The military revealed that one of the soldiers was Lieutenant-Colonel Dor Gedalia Ben Simhon, aged 32, from Beit HaShita, the commander of the 52nd Battalion, 401st Brigade. The IDF announced on Friday that four soldiers were killed while fighting in southern Lebanon, with the commander of the 52nd Battalion, 401st Brigade, Lieutenant-Colonel Dor Gedalia Ben Simhon, aged 32, among the fallen troops.

item.description: It comes a day after the US and Iran signed a deal to end their conflict, including fighting in Lebanon.

item.title: Elder fraud cases jump 61% in Israel as abuse reports rise, Welfare Ministry says
item.description: Of the cases where an elder was defrauded, 92% of the perpetrators were family members of the victim, with 48% their children and 33% their spouse. Elder fraud cases jumped by 61% in Israel over 2025, the Welfare Ministry announced on Monday in a report for World Elder Abuse Awareness Day.

item.title: Russia sentences Romanian man to 15 years for spying for Ukraine
item.description: Russia's FSB domestic intelligence service said on Friday that a Romanian citizen arrested on charges of spying for Ukraine in Russia had been sentenced to 15 years in a penal colony. The man, born in 2002, had been surveying Russian air-defence sites in the Black Sea city of Sochi on behalf of Ukraine, the FSB said in a press release.

item.description: Carl Holland, 30, died in hospital where he was being treated for a serious head injury.

item.description: TOKYO, June 19 (Reuters) - A vessel owned by a Japanese company carrying three Japanese crew members safely passed through the Strait of Hormuz on Friday and exited the Gulf, Japan's Ministry of

item.title: Over 50 detained in Turkey raids targeting opposition offices
item.description: Turkish authorities expanded raids targeting municipalities run by the main opposition Republican People's Party (CHP), detaining dozens of suspects in corruption probes in Istanbul and the southern province of Mersin, state broadcaster TRT reported on Friday. In an Istanbul-based probe, covering four cities, prosecutors ordered the detention of 47 suspects over alleged bribery, corruption and organized crime.

item.title: Clip of Iranian soldiers firing at US military helicopter is AI-generated
item.description: Iran and the United States engaged in tit-for-tat strikes in June 2026 before they reached a deal aimed at ending the Middle East war, but a video of Iranian troops shooting down a US helicopter circulating online does not show the attack. The clip shows visual errors and was flagged by an AI detection tool that it was fabricated.

item.title: Man arrested after 3-year-old injured in zoo's crocodile enclosure
item.description: British authorities have arrested a 30-year-old man on suspicion of attempted murder after a 3-year-old child ended up in a zoo's crocodile enclosure.

item.title: Israel continues strikes on Lebanon despite US-Iran agreement
item.description: Airstrikes were carried out overnight and on Friday morning on positions of the Shiite Hezbollah militia in southern Lebanon, the Israeli military has said. The military stated on X that the strikes followed Hezbollah rocket fire targeting Israeli soldiers.

item.title: Deadly Philippines quake turns seabed into shore
item.description: Arsenio Butil Jr. fell to his knees and began to pray when last week's deadly 7.8-magnitude earthquake began shaking his home on the coast of the southern Philippines.In Glan, residents were thinking about next steps, but not yet ready to attempt to rebuild their destroyed homes, said Butil Jr, the pastor.

item.title: Zimbabwe MPs pass bill to extend president's time in power
item.description: The proposal would extend Emmerson Mnangagwa's term by two years and scrap direct presidential elections.

item.title: Haifa court stops auction of yellow star, Holocaust-era documents
item.description: Yad Ezer La-Haver operates the Holocaust Museum in Haifa and manages the shelter that provides lodging and food for tens of Holocaust survivors. The Magistrates Court in Haifa issued an immediate restraining order, forbidding the auctioning of artifacts from the Holocaust, Walla reported on Wednesday.

item.title: Freed Israeli hostage Guy Gilboa-Dalal recounts sexual abuse in Gaza, urges victims to speak out
item.description: An Israeli man who said he was sexually abused while he was held hostage in the Gaza Strip is hoping to use his voice to help empower victims who have suffered similar assaults, including in conflict zones, he said in remarks ahead of the International Day for the Elimination of Sexual Violence in Conflict. Guy Gilboa-Dalal, 25, spent two years in captivity in Gaza after Palestinian militants abducted him and 250 others during the Hamas-led attacks in Israel on Oct. 7, 2023. “I feel like I have a mission to spread to the world, to use my voice and empower other victims of sexual assaults,” he said Sunday in a conversation with Israel's first lady Michal Herzog in Jerusalem.

item.title: Israel strikes Lebanon risking Trump's Iran peace deal
item.description: Israel's military said it carried out overnight strikes that continued into Friday morning targeting Hezbollah infrastructure.

item.title: Exclusive-Tata's iPhone parts factory faces India health probe after farmer contamination complaints
item.description: By Munsif Vengattil and Aditya Kalra HOSUR, India June 19 (Reuters) - An Indian state health authority is investigating how liquid discharged from Apple supplier Tata's iPhone components factory has

item.title: Old Karachi protest footage misrepresented as clashes in Pakistan-administered Kashmir
item.description: Clashes between police and members of a banned protest movement in June 2026 killed at least 22 people in Pakistan-administered Kashmir, but a clip circulating online showing officers firing on demonstrators was not filmed during the recent protests. Visual evidence indicates it was in fact recorded months ago in Karachi at protests sparked by the killing of Iran's supreme leader.

item.title: Rheinmetall boss urges rules on AI use in weapons
item.description: Rheinmetall chief executive Armin Papperger has called for global rules to ensure that humans, rather than machines using artificial intelligence (AI), continue to decide when weapons are fired in war. Papperger told dpa at the Eurosatory defence exhibition in Villepinte near Paris that technological possibilities in the defence industry were likely to expand exponentially over the next 10 years.

item.title: Several injured in Russian airstrike on Ukraine's Kharkiv
item.description: At least nine people, including four children, were injured in a heavy Russian airstrike on the Ukrainian city of Kharkiv, officials said on Friday. More than 40 private homes were damaged when guided glide bombs were dropped on the city, Kharkiv Mayor Ihor Terekhov said on Telegram.

item.title: Switzerland says planned US-Iran talks called off
item.description: A planned first round of talks between the United States and Iran on the details of a framework agreement has been called off, the Swiss foreign ministry said. "The talks at the Bürgenstock will not take place today as planned," the ministry in Bern said on Friday, without giving a reason.

item.title: Police charge a third suspect in a Melbourne synagogue arson allegedly directed by Iran
item.description: Police charged a third suspect on Friday with an arson attack on a Melbourne synagogue that was allegedly directed by Iran. The 20-year-old man was one of three masked offenders who broke into the Adass Israel Synagogue, doused the interior with flammable liquid then set it alight in the early hours of Dec. 6, 2024, a police statement alleged. The fire caused extensive damage to the synagogue and a worshipper sustained minor injuries.

item.title: No vaccine, conflict, mistrust: Ebola's return to DR Congo
item.description: Ebola is spreading again in the eastern Democratic Republic of Congo, where conflict, mistrust and the lack of a vaccine have made the outbreak hard to contain. - The DRC's 17th Ebola outbreak was officially declared on May 15 but is believed to have been spreading under the radar for weeks.

item.title: Arnold Whittall, musicologist and leading Wagner scholar
item.description: Arnold Whittall, the musicologist who has died aged 90, did not claim to know everything in his field, merely to have read everything; he was a leading figure in the study of 20th- and 21st-century music, his sharp observations and provocative challenges helping students towards a greater understanding and appreciation of the complexities of music from that time.

item.description: Every two minutes, a woman dies from complications related to pregnancy or childbirth.

item.description: When Emmerson Mnangagwa came to power in the 2017 coup that toppled Robert Mugabe, celebrations on the streets of Harare were matched by relief in chancelleries from London to Beijing.

item.title: Israeli military strikes in southern Lebanon in intense fighting as US-Iran talks postponed
item.description: Israel’s military said Friday its forces struck targets throughout southern Lebanon overnight as Hezbollah reported intense fighting in the area. Lebanon’s state-run National News Agency reported at least 16 people were killed in Israeli airstrikes. The attacks came as planned talks in Switzerland between Iran and the United States over their efforts to reach a permanent end to the Iran war were delayed.

item.description: Huxley's Birds of Prey Centre in Horsham wants people to look for Cillian, the Chilean blue eagle.

item.title: India court rejects Telegram's appeal against temporary blocking of app
item.description: A New Delhi court on Friday rejected Telegram's appeal against a temporary ban in India, a ‌setback for the messaging app which had challenged the prohibition imposed to ‌curb alleged exam-related fraud. India will conduct its national undergraduate medical entrance exam on Sunday, a month ​after it cancelled the results of the same test after allegations that the questions were leaked in advance. The unprecedented ban on Telegram until June 22 was announced this week by India's IT ministry over concerns about channels on the ‌app claiming to have questions ⁠from the upcoming exam for sale.

item.title: Fact Check: Image Was Altered With OpenAI Tools To Make German Soccer Fan Look Like Adolf Hitler
item.description: Does footage of the Germany vs. Curaçao World Cup Game show a fan in the stands who looked just like Adolf Hitler and was rooting for Germany? No, that's not true: The screenshot of the FIFA World Cup match footage...

item.description: The artwork, which once hung in Oxburgh Hall, depicts a scene from the Old Testament Book of Esther.
